title: chrysothamnus nauseosus var. washoensis
intro: Chrysothamnus nauseosus var. washoensis, also known as Wasco chamisa, is a shrub belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Wasco chamisa include its ability to reduce stress, promote relaxation, and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Wasco chamisa are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
